revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24755395338|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikoma-taisha]][[en:Ikoma Shrine]][[fr:Ikoma-jinja]][[ja:往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社]] {{Expand Japanese|往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikoma Shrine | native_name = 往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社 | image = 往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社(往馬大社) 生駒市壱分町 Ikoma-ni-imasu-ikomatsuhiko-jinja (Ikoma-taisha) 2013.8.28 - panoramio (1).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikoma Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.678259|135.703681}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Mount Ikoma|lt=Mount Ikoma|arz|جبل مونت ايكوما|ceb|Ikoma Yama|de|Ikoma (Berg)|en|Mount Ikoma|fa|ایکوما-یاما|fr|Mont Ikoma|id|Gunung Ikoma|ja|生駒山|ko|이코마산|lld|Mont Ikoma|my|အိခိုမတောင်|ro|Muntele Ikoma|th|ภูเขาอิโกมะ|zh|生駒山|qid=Q996259|1=Mount Ikoma}} | website = {{Official website|http://www.ikomataisha.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikoma Shrine'''|往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Yamato Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Taisha (shrine)|lt=taisha|en|Taisha (shrine)|ja|大社|ko|다이샤|simple|Taisha (shrine)|tr|Taisha|qid=Q11438310|1=Taisha (shrine)}} * {{ill|Shikinai Taisha|lt=Shikinai Taisha|en|Shikinai Taisha|ja|式内大社|simple|Shikinai Taisha|qid=Q134917288|1=Shikinai Taisha}} * {{ill|Q134917286|ltq=Q134917286|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|lt=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings|qid=Q135009132|1=Shrines receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11433427|ltq=Q11433427|qid=Q11433427|1=List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province}} ** Series ordinal: 35 ** P155: {{ill|Tatsutahiko & Tatsutahimeno Shrine|lt=Tatsutahiko & Tatsutahimeno Shrine|qid=Q135038847|1=Tatsutahiko & Tatsutahimeno Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q119929663|ltq=Q119929663|qid=Q119929663|1=Hegurinoiwatoko Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q109357327|ltq=Q109357327|qid=Q109357327|1=Ichibuchō}} * {{ill|Yamato Province|lt=Yamato Province|be|Ямата (правінцыя)|ca|Província de Yamato|ckb|پارێزگای یەماتۆ|cs|Provincie Jamato|de|Provinz Yamato|el|Επαρχία Γιαμάτο|en|Yamato Province|es|Provincia de Yamato|fa|ولایت یاماتو|fi|Yamaton provinssi|fr|Province de Yamato|gl|Provincia de Yamato|hak|Yamato-koet|id|Provinsi Yamato|it|Yamato (provincia)|ja|大和国|ko|야마토국|lt|Jamato žemė|nl|Yamato (provincie)|no|Yamato (provins)|pt|Província de Yamato|ro|Provincia Yamato|ru|Ямато (провинция)|simple|Yamato Province|sk|Jamato (provincia)|sr|Провинција Јамато|th|แคว้นยามาโตะ|tr|Yamato (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Ямато|ur|یاماتو صوبہ|zh_min_nan|Yamato-kok|zh_yue|大和國|zh|大和國|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}} * {{ill|Q11483501|ltq=Q11483501|qid=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.678259|135.703681}} == official website (P856) == * http://www.ikomataisha.com/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * 往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社(往馬大社) 生駒市壱分町 Ikoma-ni-imasu-ikomatsuhiko-jinja (Ikoma-taisha) 2013.8.28 - panoramio (1).jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q996259|ltq=Q996259|qid=Q996259|1=Mount Ikoma}} * {{ill|Empress Jingū|lt=Empress Jingū|ar|الإمبراطورة جينغو|arz|الامبراطوره جينجو|az|Cinqu|bcl|Empress Jingū|ca|Jingu|cs|Džingú|de|Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|es|Jingū|eu|Jingū enperatriza|fa|امپراتریس جینگو|fr|Jingū|hr|Jingū (japanska carica)|hu|Dzsingú japán császárnő|id|Permaisuri Jingū|it|Jingū|ja|神功皇后|ka|ძინგუ|ko|진구 황후|nl|Jingu|pl|Jingū|pt|Jingu|ru|Императрица Дзингу|sh|Carica Jingū|simple|Empress Jingū|si|ජින්ගූ අධිරාජිනිය|sl|Cesarica Džingu|sv|Jingu Kogo|te|రాణి జింగూ|th|จักรพรรดินีจิงงู|tl|Emperatris Jingū|tr|Jingū (imparatoriçe)|uk|Імператриця Дзінґу|uz|Imperator Jingū|vi|Thiên hoàng Jingū|zh_min_nan|Sîn-kong Hông-hō͘|zh_yue|神功皇后|zh|神功皇后|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}} * {{ill|Q179971|ltq=Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}} * {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین /m/02pyzb3 == image of entrance (P9721) == * Ikoma Taisya 02.jpg {{commons category|Category:Ikoma-taisha}} [[EN:Ikoma Shrine]] [[FR:Ikoma-jinja]] [[JA:往馬坐伊古麻都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q246479}} == Japanese content == {{Source stated|date=November 2019}} {{Shrine | Name = Ikoma-ni-imasu Ikomatsuhiko Shrine | Image = [[File:Ikoma Taisya 02.jpg|270px|Ikoma Taisha torii]]
Torii | Location = {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikoma City|Q328610|qid=Q328610|1=Ikoma, Nara}}{{ill|lt=Ichibu Town|Q109357327|qid=Q109357327|1=Ichibuchō}}1527-1 | Coordinates = {{Two-tier wiki coordinates degrees minutes seconds|34|40|41|N|135|42|13|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = Ikomatsuhiko-no-Kami
Ikomatsuhime-no-Kami
Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto
Ashinaka Tsuhiko no Mikoto
Hondawake no Mikoto
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi no Taka-nukahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|lt=Okinaga no Sukune no Ōkimi|Q11492327|qid=Q11492327|1=Okinaga no Sukunu no miko}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Major, 2 seats)
Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Alternative name = Ikoma Taisha | Main hall style = {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} | Annual festival = Second Sunday of October (Fire Festival) | Sacred object = {{ill|lt=Mount Ikoma|Q996259|qid=Q996259|1=Mount Ikoma}} | Map = {{Location map|Japan Nara|label=Ikoma Taisha|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200}} }} [[File:Ikoma-ni-imasu-ikomatsuhiko-jinja (Ikoma-taisha) Ikoma City Ichibu Town 2013.8.28 - panoramio (1).jpg|alt=Haiden and Honden|thumbnail|243x243px|Haiden and Honden]] '''Ikoma-ni-imasu Ikomatsuhiko Shrine''' (いこまにいますいこまつひこじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Ikoma City|Q328610|qid=Q328610|1=Ikoma, Nara}}, {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|qid=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. It is commonly known as '''Ikoma Taisha'''. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities – Ikomatsuhiko-no-Kami (deity of {{ill|lt=Mount Ikoma|Q996259|qid=Q996259|1=Mount Ikoma}}), Ikomatsuhime-no-Kami (deity of Mount Ikoma), Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), Ashinaka Tsuhiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}), Hondawake no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi no Taka-nukahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (mother of Empress Jingū), and Okinaga no Sukune no Ōkimi no Mikoto (father of Empress Jingū) Ikomatsuhiko-no-Kami and Ikomatsuhime-no-Kami have been worshipped since ancient times as deities governing fire. The fire-drill wood (hikiri-gi) used to kindle the sacred fire for the {{ill|lt=Daijōsai|Q11433653|qid=Q11433653|1=Daijosai}} has traditionally been presented by this shrine, and the shrine’s fire-drill wood was also used for the Daijōsai of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Emperor Akihito|1=UNKNOWN}}. Texts such as the *Hokuzanshō*, *Gengōki*, and *Kisōki* refer to the shrine’s deity as the “Hikiri-gi no Kami” (God of the Fire-Drill Wood). When determining the two provinces of Yuki and Suki for the Daijōsai (the ritual in which newly harvested rice is first offered to the deities at an imperial accession), divination is performed by burning a turtle shell, and it was customary to use fire-drill wood presented from the Ikoma Shrine on that occasion. This fire-drill wood is called hahaka, corresponding to what is today known as Uwamisozakura (Prunus grayana). The annual festival held on the second Sunday of October (formerly {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|qid=Q2921|1=October 10}}) is called the “Fire Deity Festival,” and together with the Wind Deity Festival of {{ill|lt=Tatsuta Taisha|Q246455|qid=Q246455|1=Tatsuta Taisha}} and the Ōimi Festival of {{ill|lt=Hirose Taisha|Q705281|qid=Q705281|1=Hirose Taisha}}, it has been revered by the imperial court since ancient times. The Fire Deity Festival is a traditional event in which young men carrying torches race down stone steps, and it has been designated an {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=UNKNOWN}} by the Nara Prefectural Board of Education. === History === The date of foundation is unknown, but the shrine has been enshrined at this location as the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} (tutelary deity) of the seventeen villages of the Ikoma Valley. Along with {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|qid=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} and {{ill|lt=Isonokami Shrine|Q708859|qid=Q708859|1=Isonokami Jingū}}, it is one of Japan’s leading ancient shrines that enshrine a kannabi ({{ill|lt=Mount Ikoma|Q996259|qid=Q996259|1=Mount Ikoma}}) as the sacred object of worship within {{ill|lt=Nara Prefecture|Q131287|qid=Q131287|1=Nara Prefecture}}. The sacred grove (chinju no mori) covering the shrine grounds is designated a {{ill|lt=Natural Monument|Q23790|qid=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Nara Prefecture. The oldest literary reference appears in the *Sōkoku Fudoki*, in the entry for the 3rd year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Yūryaku|Q357235|qid=Q357235|1=Emperor Yūryaku}} ({{ill|lt=458|Q30922|qid=Q30922|1=458}}), which mentions “Ikomatsuhiko Shrine.” In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as “Ikoma-ni-imasu Ikomatsuhiko Shrine, two seats” in {{ill|lt=Yamato Province|Q907495|qid=Q907495|1=Yamato Province}}, {{ill|lt=Heguri District|Q11483501|qid=Q11483501|1=Heguri district}}, ranked as a major shrine and recorded as receiving offerings at the Tsukinami and Niiname festivals. Of the two seats of Ikoma-ni-imasu Ikomatsuhiko Shrine, one also received offerings for prayers for rain. During the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, there were said to be eleven {{ill|lt=jingū-ji|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} (temple-shrine complexes). Originally, the main hall enshrined two seats as the tutelary deities of the land, but in the {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}, devotion to {{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}, the guardian deity of the warrior class, flourished, and five Hachiman deities were enshrined together, resulting in the present seven seats. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji era|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}, due to the {{ill|lt=separation of Shinto and Buddhism|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, the jingū-ji were abolished and the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}}. === Shrine precincts === * Main halls (7 buildings) – Seven connected {{ill|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|qid=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} cypress-bark-roofed main halls stand here. Ikomatsuhiko-no-Kami is enshrined at the center, with Ikomatsuhime-no-Kami to the right. From the left, the order is: Okinaga no Sukune no Ōkimi no Mikoto, Okinaga Tarashihime no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Empress Jingū|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}), Hondawake no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}), Ikomatsuhiko-no-Kami, Ikomatsuhime-no-Kami, Ashinaka Tsuhiko no Mikoto ({{ill|lt=Emperor Chūai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}), and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Katsuragi no Taka-nukahime no Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. * Norito hall * Worship hall (haiden) * Eireiden – It is said that a golden memorial tablet of {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Empress Shōtoku|1=UNKNOWN}} was buried at this site. * Kannon Hall – A remnant of {{ill|lt=Shinbutsu-shūgō|Q2279088|qid=Q2279088|1=Shinbutsu-shūgō}}. It enshrines a statue of {{ill|lt=Eleven-Headed Kannon|Q11404731|qid=Q11404731|1=Ekādaśamukha}} (traditionally attributed to Unkei), the honji-butsu of Empress Jingū. Few shrines retain Buddhist halls. * Offering hall (shinsenjo) * Purification hall (saikan) * Ritual implements storehouse * Rōmon gate – In fact, it is a karamon gate. * Yakura * Kōza * North seat * South seat * Gagaku hall * Misogi place * North mikoshi storehouse * South mikoshi storehouse * Shrine office === Auxiliary and subordinate shrines === * Haraedo Shrine – Deity: Seoritsuhime-no-Kami * Suijin Shrine – Deity: Mizuhanome-no-Kami * Inari Shrine – Deity: {{ill|lt=Uga-no-Mitama-no-Kami|Q795080|qid=Q795080|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ugajin}} * Ebisu Shrine – Deity: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}-no-Kami * Northern subordinate shrines ** Ōyamatsumi Shrine – Deity: {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} ** Kasuga Shrine – Deity: {{jalink|Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mik === Subsidiary Shrines === * Haraedo Shrine – Enshrined deity: Seoritsuhime-no-Kami * Suijin Shrine – Enshrined deity: Mizuhame-no-Kami * Inari Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Uga-no-Kami|Q795080|qid=Q795080|qid=Q3080728|lt=Ukanomitama-no-Kami|1=Ugajin}} * Ebisu Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|Q8009611|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}-no-Kami * Northern Subsidiary Shrines ** Ōyamatsumi Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi|Q386563|qid=Q386563|qid=Q386563|lt=Ōyamatsumi-no-Kami|1=Ōyamatsumi}} ** Kasuga Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}} ** Shinmei Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}} ** Emperor Nintoku Shrine – Enshrined deity: Ōsasagi-no-Kami ** Toyouke-hime Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Toyouke-hime|Q2983724|qid=Q2983724|lt=Toyouke-hime-no-Kami|1=Toyouke-hime}} * Southern Subsidiary Shrines ** Tsukuyomi Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi|Q595520|qid=Q595520|qid=Q595520|lt=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto|1=Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto}} ** Sarutahiko Shrine – Enshrined deity: {{ill|lt=Sarutahiko|Q3090037|qid=Q3090037|lt=Sarutahiko-no-Kami|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} ** Sumiyoshi Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|Q1114013|qid=Q1114013|lt=Sokotsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Nakatsutsuo-no-Mikoto, Uwatsutsuo-no-Mikoto|1=Sumiyoshi sanjin}} ** Izanagi Shrine – Enshrined deities: {{ill|lt=Izanagi|Q813858|qid=Q813858|lt=Izanagi-no-Mikoto|1=Izanagi}}, {{ill|lt=Izanami|Q682306|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami-no-Mikoto|1=Izanami}} === Outside the Precincts === * Kōra Shrine – Enshrined deities: Ōtomo no Takei-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}-no-Mikoto * Kasuga Shrine (Separate Hall) – Enshrined deities: Ugayafukiaezu-no-Mikoto, {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}}-no-Kami, Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto, Ōhirume-no-Muchi-no-Kami === Cultural Properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Ikoma Mandala, color on silk – {{ill|lt=Kamakura period|Q236205|qid=Q236205|1=Kamakura period}}. In the collection of the {{ill|lt=Nara National Museum|Q147312|qid=Q147312|1=Nara National Museum}}[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/2105 Ikoma Mandala, color on silk] Agency for Cultural Affairs. Accessed December 1, 2020.. === Nara Prefecture–Designated Tangible Cultural Properties === * Ikoma Mandala, color on silk – {{ill|lt=Muromachi period|Q334845|qid=Q334845|1=Muromachi period}}[http://www.pref.nara.jp/secure/143530/kenshiteiichiran.pdf “List of Cultural Properties Designated by Nara Prefecture” (PDF)] Nara Prefecture. Accessed December 1, 2020.. Deposited with the Nara National Museum. === Nara Prefecture–Designated Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * Fire-Taking Ritual of Ikomataisha Shrine === Nara Prefecture–Designated Natural Monuments === * Shrine forest of Ikomataisha Shrine === On-site Information === ; Access * {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Ikoma Line|Q900218|qid=Q900218|1=Ikoma Line}} {{ill|lt=Ichibu Station|Q5986106|qid=Q5986106|1=Ichibu Station (Nara)}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Daini Hanna Toll Road|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ichibu Ramp|1=UNKNOWN}} / {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ose Tollgate|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=National Route 168|1=UNKNOWN}} {{ill|lt=Ichibu Bypass|Q11352447|qid=Q11352447|1=A minute bypass}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External Links === * [https://ikomataisha.or.jp/ Official website of Ikomataisha Shrine] * “Six Prefectural Cultural Properties Designated, Including the Former Shōhōin Family Residence and Ikomataisha’s ‘Ikoma Fire Festival’,” *Asahi Shimbun*, April 13, 2011 {{Shinto sidebar}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikoma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikoma Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Nara Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:History of Ikoma City]] [[Category:生駒市の建築物]]
